original: "+(0,it.zI)($I||($I=(0,et.Z)(["Sparks fizz, and a primitive speaker crackles to life from the nearest shipwreck. “Kzzt - CLOSED for new business. Further intrusion will be considered a HOSTILE ACT.” It cuts back off with a startling crackle. You glance north, noting other hulls. Some are rusted and of local manufacture while others sport holes blasted through their scorched hulls. Those are unanimously of modern, UGC make. They appear to be arranged by their state of disrepair - the more intact the hull, the further to the north the vessel has been positioned. The speaker interrupts your thoughts, “Kzzt-eriously, we’re not open right now. I don’t care if you’re by yourself. Don’t.” It clicks back off. Someone really doesn’t want you coming north."])))

original: "+(0,it.zI)(tY||(tY=(0,et.Z)(["One of the more intact wrecks to the north abruptly whirrs to life, its heavy servomotors purring as effectively as when they were fresh from the factory. The capital-class turret rotating in your direction looks similarly well maintained, even if the ship housing it is a mere piece of a much grander whole. Power thrums. There’s a glow inside the barrels. The aim is shallow, just short of you, but it’s a damn big gun. You hop back with all the speed you can muster, and just in time too. A blast of radioactive dirt and pulverized glass washes over you before you’re even aware that the weapon fired, digging a fresh, eight-foot crater into the ground. The Codex’s geiger counter is going crazy, but it quiets before the dust finishes settling. Another speaker, somewhere closer declares. “Kzzt-arning shot. We’re closed. Please don’t make me murder you. You can leave.” It turns off. Five seconds later, it’s on again. “Kzzt-ou look like you can understand me at least, so maybe I won’t have to kill a confused alien today. Yay me! Go southeast - and watch out for Hyrax. If you make it far enough east, then turn north, you’ll be on your way to shineglass. Maybe you can find what you’re looking for that way.” It cuts off once more. You should definitely not step any further north, even if there’s some nice looking shipwrecks over there."])))

original: You shake your head. “10 K is outrageous.” For that price, you could get a JoyCo hoverbike of your own, and you say as much. You neglect to mention that JoyCo’s hoverbikes are well documented deathtraps, unlikely to survive more than a pair of seasons on a pastoral planet, let alone on an irradiated hellscape. “Half that is more than reasonable.” You fold your arms across your [pc.fullChest], confident in your haggling skills. The nuka laughs. “All right then. 5 K.” She looks back over her shoulder at the bike. “Honestly I bet you’re low-balling me.” Her head-tendril twitches. “No Gasm-Corp or whatever bike is going to survive Phaedra, let alone multiple trips, but you’re still a more trustworthy sort than the raiders and cultists I usually bump into. Maybe offworld evil’s just different, I don’t know, but yeah, 5 K. Let’s do it.” She holds out her hand expectantly. You transfer

original: "+(0,it.zI)(EY||(EY=(0,et.Z)(["The familiar, throaty roar of Gayll’s hoverbike’s screams by, but it’s not alone - a deeper, bassier rumble chases from behind. You look back over your shoulder in time to see a hyraxi raider on his junker bike, spewing clouds of dust and polluted, black exhaust in his wake. Over his shoulder, he’s hefting something truly bizarre: a collection of rails and rings that crackle with alarming amounts of electricity. The hyena-like alien cackles madly and depresses the trigger. Your vision whites out. When it returns, they’re a purple-black scar across the middle of your sight where the raider beam crossed. Spots dance at the edges as your enhanced immune system works double time to save your injured retinas. Gayll’s engine sputters to a stop somewhere in the distance... but the whooshing wind of her transit is getting louder. Rocks kick up as the biker skids to a stop a few feet away, hooting madly. Then he looks up. He gasps in alarm and drops his smoking, improvised EMP cannon, pointing a gleaming silver streak in the sky that’s rapidly approaching your position. Gayll screams from the back of her crippled mount. She yanks back on a lever, and small maneuvering wings pop out of the sides, nowhere enough to keep her aloft but plenty for steering into the least deadly patch of grind. She sails past one more time, low enough for the falling vessel’s shockwave to ruffle ",". Her mask of rage deepens to murderous fury, and the nuka rips a plasma gun out of a side-mounted holster to spray at the fiendish land-pirate on her way by, actually landing a few hits on his bike before her floating chopper’s nose kisses the ground and begins tumbling like a pipe rolling down a cliffside. Gayll’s body ragdolls into the air as it’s flung clear, tumbling thirty meters further than the rest of the wreck. She looks like she’s still breathing, for now. The hyraxi looks over at you with an absolutely delighted grin. “Yeehee... flying bitch is finally gonna pay for hassling us. You watch, bear witness to hyraxi glory over slipperiest foe!” He grabs a metallic silver collar from his bike’s storage compartment. Whatever he has planned for Gayll seems permanent. If you don’t intervene, this may be the last time you ever see the cocky nuka. Do you challenge the hyraxi warboy?"])),pc.hasHair()?"your [pc.hair]":"the hyraxi raider’s hair")
